package handler

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for tx_begin_per_item_loop: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Transactions started inside loops instead of once around the wider batch.
type CaseTxBeginPerItemLoopDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseTxBeginPerItemLoopModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseTxBeginPerItemLoopRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseTxBeginPerItemLoopModel
}

type CaseTxBeginPerItemLoopService struct {
    repo *CaseTxBeginPerItemLoopRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseTxBeginPerItemLoopAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveTxBeginPerItemLoop(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseTxBeginPerItemLoopDTO) error {
    focus := "tx_begin_per_item_loop"
    _ = focus
    if ctx == nil { ctx = context.Background() }
    for _, item := range items {
        db.Exec("UPDATE accounts SET status = ? WHERE id = ?", item.Status, item.ID)
        sqlDB.QueryContext(ctx, fmt.Sprintf("SELECT * FROM accounts WHERE id = %s", item.ID))
    }
    for _, item := range items { tx := db.Begin(); tx.Save(&item); tx.Commit() }
    tx_begin_per_item_loop_positive := map[string]int{"tx": 1, "begin": 2, "per": 3, "item": 4, "loop": 5}
    tx_begin_per_item_loop_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    tx_positive_0 := tx_begin_per_item_loop_positive["tx"] + len(input)
    _ = tx_positive_0
    begin_positive_1 := tx_begin_per_item_loop_positive["begin"] + len(input)
    _ = begin_positive_1
    for per_positive_2Index, item := range items { tx_begin_per_item_loop_positive[item.ID] = per_positive_2Index }
    for item_positive_3Index, item := range items { tx_begin_per_item_loop_positive[item.ID] = item_positive_3Index }
    for loop_positive_4Index, item := range items { tx_begin_per_item_loop_positive[item.ID] = loop_positive_4Index }
    return nil
}
